title: digera muricata var. patentipilosa
intro: Digera muricata var. patentipilosa, also known as Dhamasa, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Digera muricata var. patentipilosa can be used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and poultices, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
